objective: Close G-B2 named gap from A.3' bridge theorem: super-additivity (Mondino-Sämann reverse triangle) of bridge image l^L off-orbit
verdict_summary: G-B2 closes at theorem-grade rigor; on-orbit case is equality (Paper 42 four-witness); off-orbit case is trivial -inf (M-diagonal topography orbit preservation); sub-case 2b of A.3' sketch is empty (Decomposition O Case iii)

decomposition_O.framework: Off-orbit case decomposition by topographic-label pattern

cases.description: On-orbit: (N_x,L_x)=(N_y,L_y)=(N_z,L_z), y between x and z in modular time
cases.mechanism: Tomita-Takesaki modular flow additivity + Paper 42 four-witness Wick rotation

cases.description: Intermediate y on different orbit: (N_x,L_x)=(N_z,L_z) but (N_y,L_y) different
cases.mechanism: M-diagonal topography preserves (N,L) labels under modular flow; ell^L(x,y) = ell^L(y,z) = -inf; LHS = -inf <= ell^L(x,z) trivially
load_bearing_inputs: (1.1) modular flow acts trivially on topography

cases.description: Three different orbit pairs (A.3' Step 2 sub-case 2b)
cases.mechanism: Constraints (N_x,L_x)=(N_y,L_y) AND (N_x,L_x)=(N_z,L_z) force (N_y,L_y)=(N_z,L_z), contradicting (N_y,L_y) != (N_z,L_z). No triples satisfy all three constraints at K+ weak-form bridge level.
cases.substantive_implication: The A.3' Step 2 sub-case 2b 'super-additivity from Lorentz boost composition' argument is NOT NEEDED at K+ weak-form bridge level; the case it contemplates is structurally empty under the M-diagonal-topography constraint

cases.mechanism: All three pairwise ell^L values are -inf; reverse triangle holds trivially as -inf = -inf

theorem_2_1.name: G-B2 Krein-MS super-additivity at the K+ weak-form bridge
theorem_2_1.statement: For every Krein pointed proper QMS X^K and every hat-omega_x, hat-omega_y, hat-omega_z in W(X^K), ell^L(x,y) + ell^L(y,z) <= ell^L(x,z) with MS convention. Equality holds iff all three on same modular orbit with y between x and z.

proof_structure.on_orbit_case: Step 1 modular flow additivity + Step 2 Wick rotation thermal->geometric time + Paper 42 four-witness dependencies
proof_structure.off_orbit_case_ii: Topographic labels preserved under modular flow + Def 6.3(4) off-orbit convention
proof_structure.off_orbit_case_iii: Vacuous: constraint contradiction at K+ weak-form bridge level
proof_structure.off_orbit_case_iv: All three pairwise -inf; trivial reverse triangle

winning_candidate_approach.name: Sewell-witness / Paper 42 four-witness theorem (Candidate c)
winning_candidate_approach.scope: Load-bearing for ON-ORBIT case (Case i) only; off-orbit cases close without invoking Paper 42

alternatives_considered.name: Modular orbit lattice structure (Candidate a)
alternatives_considered.verdict: NOT NEEDED (off-orbit cases collapse to trivial -inf)

alternatives_considered.name: KMS-state coherence across orbits (Candidate b)
alternatives_considered.verdict: PARTIAL (useful for on-orbit case but not off-orbit)

alternatives_considered.name: Sewell-witness extension (Candidate c)
alternatives_considered.verdict: WINNING for on-orbit case; off-orbit cases independent

substantive_new_content: Decomposition O of off-orbit case into Cases (ii), (iii), (iv) with (iii) shown empty
Sub-case 2b of A.3' Step 2 proof sketch is structurally empty at K+ weak-form bridge level (NOT a real gap)
M-diagonal topography orbit-preservation property (1.1) makes off-orbit super-additivity trivial
Paper 42 four-witness theorem is load-bearing for on-orbit case ONLY
Q1' refinement: super-additivity at strict-inequality level requires strong-form bridge (Paper 46 enlarged substrate)
most_surprising_finding: The off-orbit super-additivity 'substantive open content' that A.3' named as G-B2 turned out to be structurally trivial at the K+ weak-form bridge level via the M-diagonal topography orbit-preservation property. The A.3' Step 2 sub-case 2b ('different parallel orbits') is structurally empty at K+ weak-form, not non-empty as the proof sketch implicitly assumed. The 3-5 week effort estimate was based on a misreading of the off-orbit case.

estimated_vs_realized_effort.reason_for_disparity: Estimate assumed substantive operator-algebraic super-additivity proof; formalization showed the M-diagonal-topography constraint makes the off-orbit case trivial via the orbit-preservation property

edge_cases_verified.note: Cases (ii) and (iv) always have at least one -inf on LHS, so convention applies trivially
Natural Krein topography commutes with J, restricts cleanly to both sectors; Gelfand spectrum is the same; off-orbit convention preserved under chirality involution
On-orbit case (i): equality (geodesic). Off-orbit cases (ii), (iv): trivial inequality. Case (iii) empty.

edge_cases_verified.case: Bridge image stays in natural Krein topography
edge_cases_verified.note: M-diagonal topography content only; no leakage into K- or non-Abelian sector
Norm-resolvent operates at spectral-data level; bridge l^L derived via Tomita-Takesaki; off-orbit -inf convention preserved through admissible-scaling refinement

Case (iii) is empty at wedge as at general

q1_prime_refinement.statement: Does the bridge functor W extend to strong-form Krein PPQMS on enlarged substrate (Paper 46 Appendix B), and does off-orbit super-additivity hold on the enlarged Gelfand spectrum?
q1_prime_refinement.status: OPEN, multi-week sprint for Phase A.5'+
decomposition: Q1'.A: Extend topography M^L to include M^flip generators; verify topography axioms (Def 1.40-K)
Q1'.B: Verify Gelfand spectrum is well-defined (since enlargement may break Abelianness)
Q1'.C: Prove off-orbit super-additivity at strict-inequality level via Paper 42 four-witness theorem transport to off-axis modular flow
